title: Universal SDD UI PIM Meta-Schema (Full Spec)
description: SDD Production Line: Semantic Intent Specification with Interaction Logic, Contractual Linkage, and UX Design Principles.

ux_principle_ref.description: A UX design principle or heuristic reference. May be a BDD Scenario title (in docs/02-design-specs/behavior-specs/user/) OR one of the canonical UX principle identifiers: 'nielsen:visibility', 'nielsen:feedback', 'nielsen:control', 'nielsen:consistency', 'nielsen:error-prevention', 'nielsen:recognition', 'nielsen:flexibility', 'nielsen:aesthetic', 'nielsen:recovery', 'nielsen:help', 'gestalt:proximity', 'gestalt:similarity', 'gestalt:closure', 'visual-hierarchy:size', 'visual-hierarchy:weight', 'visual-hierarchy:color', 'fitts-law', 'hicks-law', 'millers-law', 'f-pattern'.

feedback_config.description: Declarative feedback configuration for action results. Ensures every user action communicates success or failure (Nielsen #1: Visibility of System Status).

on_success.description: Feedback shown when the action completes successfully.

type.description: 'toast' = transient notification; 'dialog' = blocking message dialog; 'inline' = inline status text next to the element.

message_key.description: i18n key or literal text for the success message.

on_error.description: Feedback shown when the action fails (system error or validation failure).

type.description: 'toast' = transient notification; 'dialog' = blocking error dialog; 'inline' = inline error message.

message_key.description: i18n key or literal text for the error message.

column_def.description: Definition of a single table column, including optional sort support.

field.description: The data field name this column maps to (must match the entity attribute name from the UML model).

label.description: Column header label (use canonical Glossary term).

sortable.description: If true, the column header renders a sort control. Defaults to false. Apply Fitts' Law — sort trigger must be clearly clickable.

default_sort.description: If set, this column is the default sort column on initial render.

id.description: The unique functional identifier for this element. Used as a key for State Management and as a 'target_id' for interactions.

abstract_type.description: The Web Components-based semantic category. Dictates the core behavior and role of the component in a platform-independent manner.

semantic_variant.description: A hint for the Transpiler/Jig to select a specific implementation variant. Common values: 'primary', 'secondary', 'destructive', 'icon' (Trigger); 'modal', 'drawer', 'tooltip', 'confirm-dialog', 'error-dialog', 'success-dialog' (Overlay); 'date-picker', 'text', 'number', 'search', 'textarea' (Field); 'data-table', 'summary-table' (Table); 'page', 'card', 'panel' (Container); 'form-section', 'list-section', 'summary-section' (Section); 'kpi-grid', 'card-grid' (Grid); 'action-bar', 'breadcrumb', 'filter-bar' (Stack); 'dropdown', 'radio', 'checkbox', 'multi-select' (Selection).

label.description: The human-readable label or the internationalization (i18n) key for the component's display name.

data_ref.description: Contractual Pointer: References either (1) the exact 'operationId' in docs/02-design-specs/api-contracts/openapi.yaml for REST mutations and single-entity reads, OR (2) the GraphQL resolver method name from docs/02-design-specs/uml/*_contract.puml for collection reads (e.g., 'listGradeRecords', 'listClasses'). Collection GET operations are DEPRECATED_TO_GRAPHQL — use the resolver method name, not the deprecated REST operationId. The actual GraphQL query document is dynamic and does not need to be pre-defined; only the resolver name is contractually anchored.

behavior_ref.description: Contractual Pointer: References either (1) a Scenario title in docs/02-design-specs/behavior-specs/user/*.feature that defines the UI logic/validation, OR (2) a canonical UX principle identifier (e.g., 'nielsen:feedback', 'fitts-law', 'gestalt:proximity'). See $defs.ux_principle_ref for the full list of valid UX principle identifiers. Multiple principles may be comma-separated.

ux_hints.description: Structured UX design annotations providing layout, cognitive-load, and accessibility guidance derived from established design laws. These are declarative hints for the Transpiler/Jig — not behavioral logic.

principles.description: List of UX principle identifiers that govern this element's design. Used to guide the Transpiler in component selection and layout decisions.

max_visible_options.description: Hick's Law: Maximum number of choices to display before collapsing into a 'More' overflow. Recommended ≤ 7.

group_size_hint.description: Miller's Law: Suggested grouping size for related items (e.g., fields per form section, columns per table). Recommended 5–9.

primary_action_placement.description: F-Pattern guidance: primary CTAs and most important information should anchor to 'top-left'. Avoid placing CTAs at 'bottom-right' unless secondary.

gestalt_group.description: Gestalt Proximity/Similarity: A logical group name shared by related elements (e.g., 'grade-inputs', 'filter-controls'). Elements with the same gestalt_group should be rendered in close visual proximity.

feedback.description: Action feedback configuration. REQUIRED for all Trigger elements that perform mutations (create, update, delete, import, export, calculate). Ensures Nielsen's Heuristic #1 (Visibility) and #9 (Help users recover from errors). System-level errors (5xx) must always use 'dialog' type.

confirm_delete.description: Destructive action confirmation gate. REQUIRED for all delete/remove Triggers. Forces the user to type a confirmation keyword before the action is submitted, preventing accidental data loss.

keyword_label.description: Instruction text shown to the user (e.g., 'Please type the class name to confirm deletion').

keyword_value.description: The dynamic data field whose value the user must type verbatim (e.g., '{{class.name}}'). Use mustache-style template syntax to reference the entity being deleted.

columns.description: Column definitions for Table elements. Enables column-level sort control (Fitts' Law: sort trigger must be large enough to click; Miller's Law: limit visible columns to 7±2). Only valid when abstract_type is 'Table'.

interactions.description: Defines declarative event-driven logic between components within the same UI scope.

on_event.description: The UI event that triggers this interaction.

target_id.description: The 'id' of another ui_element in this schema that will react to this event.

behavior_ref.description: Points to a BDD Scenario title OR a canonical UX principle identifier explaining the transformation or data-flow during this interaction.

required.description: Indicates if this field is required for validation.

children.description: Recursive list of nested ui_elements. Enables complex tree-based UI layouts.

ui_id.description: Unique identifier for this specific UI specification file (e.g., 'grade-entry-board', 'semester-dashboard').

domain_module.description: Domain containment. Must align with the Package/Namespace defined in UML models and the Glossary.

root_element.description: The single entry point for the UI component tree. All visual elements are nested within this node.
